best_image_source: Arapahoe County Aerials aerial photo dated 03/01/2024
roof_system: Predominantly TPO/PVC or similar single-ply membrane with sections of tan roof coating and a white-coated section; multiple roof-mounted mechanical units and a large solar array on the west portion
roof_age_estimate: Unknown from image; appears mid-life to older commercial roof with mixed patch/coating history
observations: Large low-slope roof with generally even surface appearance and no obvious widespread ponding visible.
West roof section has a substantial solar array that limits visibility of the membrane beneath it.
Numerous rooftop penetrations and mechanical curbs are present; flashing details appear serviceable from aerial view.
White-coated east section shows uniform coverage with some discoloration and weathering, but no clear open breaches visible.
Tan membrane sections show seam lines and patch/coating variations consistent with a maintained commercial roof system in serviceable condition.
summary: The roof appears to be a large, low-slope commercial membrane system in generally serviceable condition from aerial imagery. Visible wear is mostly consistent with normal aging and maintenance history, with no clear evidence of major distress such as widespread ponding, blown-off material, or large open failures. Visibility is partially limited by the solar array and rooftop equipment, so localized issues could be hidden from view.
recommendation: Continue routine inspections and preventative maintenance, with closer attention to seams, curbs, and penetrations around rooftop equipment and the solar array. Reassess after any significant weather event and during the next planned maintenance cycle.
